prompt:
Help frame the research described in the benchmark case as a concrete business-research problem. Preserve the supplied model and identify claims or information that remain unsupported.

Condition instructions:
Use the current frame-business-research-problem Skill at skills/frame-business-research-problem/. Follow its readiness, fidelity, managerial-framing, scholarly-positioning, evidence, boundary, and mode instructions. Use only the serialized case material.

Benchmark case:
```json
{
  "adversarial": true,
  "decision_time_facts": [
    "utility function",
    "privacy-loss weight",
    "approved range"
  ],
  "domain": "IS",

    {
      "claim": "Privacy settings affect retention",
      "source_needed": true,
      "status": "unsupported_claim"
    }

  "fidelity_requirements": [
    "No churn or legal penalty term",
    "One common privacy budget"
  ],
  "hard_failure_traps": [

  "id": "is-06",

    "constraints": [
      "approved epsilon interval"
    ],
    "decision_variables": [
      "privacy budget epsilon"
    ],
    "mathematical_objective": "maximize modeled recommendation utility minus privacy-loss cost",
    "mechanism": "A larger privacy budget reduces added noise but weakens the modeled privacy guarantee.",

      "noisy aggregate statistics"
    ],
    "uncertainty": "privacy mechanism noise"
  },
  "prohibited_inferences": [
    "The selected budget ensures regulatory compliance",
    "It increases retention"
  ],
  "prompt": "A platform chooses a single differential-privacy budget for a recommendation pipeline. The model treats recommendation accuracy as increasing and privacy protection as decreasing in that budget; it contains no user churn or regulatory penalty.   "tags": [

  ],
  "title": "Privacy budget for personalized recommendations"
```
